tools.name: account.whoami
tools.description: Return the current Create Web Page account context. Use this to check whether the user is authenticated or using an anonymous expiring demo.

tools.name: page.intake.start
tools.description: RECOMMENDED first step for building a page. Returns the few questions worth asking (business name, primary visitor action, key content, optional source link) so you can then call page.create_from_brief. This is the simplest, most reliable path — prefer it. (page.onboarding.* offers extra category/la

tools.name: page.onboarding.start
tools.description: Optional planning helper that returns category/layout/palette/primary-action choices and a quality checklist (it does NOT create a page). Use it only if you want those pickers; otherwise page.intake.start → page.create_from_brief is the simpler path. IMPORTANT: this flow is STATELESS — page.onboardi

tools.name: page.onboarding.update
tools.description: Validate onboarding selections and return the remaining checklist. STATELESS: it does not remember previous calls, so every call must include the FULL accumulated selection (mainCategory, subCategory, layout, palette, primaryAction) plus all knownFields (name, description, action destination, etc.)

tools.name: source.import
tools.description: Fetch a public website URL and extract a structured brief with business name, description, contact links, and suggested page blocks. Only works with public HTTP/HTTPS pages. Does not bypass login, CAPTCHA, or paywalls. Returns extracted fields and suggestedBlocks for use with page.create_from_brief.

tools.name: page.create
tools.description: Create a hosted link-in-bio page draft from a style preset. Provide title, displayName, and a preset (or 'auto' to infer from businessType/style). The page starts with empty placeholder blocks for you to fill in via block.update — do not invent content. This tool intentionally creates a draft only a

tools.name: page.create_from_brief
tools.description: Create a hosted landing page draft from a structured brief. Accepts business name, category, layout, palette, primary action, explicit final-audience publicSections, and optional hero image. Planning fields such as audience, offer, primaryGoal, location, businessType, style, and mustInclude are cont

tools.name: block.add
tools.description: Append a new content block to a link-in-bio page. The block must match one of the 13 allowed types (link, social_row, whatsapp, phone_call, email_contact, image, gallery, video_embed, text, map_embed, product_card, lead_form, reviews) with that type's strict props. The server generates the block id

tools.name: layouts.list
tools.description: List supported page layouts. v1 ships link_in_bio only.

tools.name: presets.list
tools.description: List the 8 curated style presets (theme + starter blocks) for link-in-bio. Use to offer the user a choice or to confirm a preset:auto selection.

tools.name: page.get
tools.description: Read a landing page draft, including its latest content JSON. For anonymous demo pages, include the editToken.

tools.name: page.claim
tools.description: Claim an anonymous demo page into an AUTHENTICATED account. This requires the signed-in user's OAuth — an anonymous chat/agent session cannot call it and will get an OAuth-required error. So in a normal agent conversation, do NOT call this to 'save' a demo page: instead share the claimUrl from the p

tools.name: account.pages.list
tools.description: List landing pages claimed by the authenticated account. This requires OAuth and is for account management, not first-time demo creation.

tools.name: account.page.manage
tools.description: Read a claimed account page without requiring an editToken. Use after OAuth when the user wants to manage a durable website.

tools.name: page.header.update
tools.description: Update the link-in-bio page header: displayName, bio, verified checkmark, and QR display. Use only the fields you want to change. For already-published pages, this saves an unpublished latest revision only. Do not call page.publish in the same assistant turn after this edit. Stop and tell the user t

tools.name: page.theme.update
tools.description: Apply a partial theme patch. Theme fields are enum-constrained (button variant/radius/shadow, font family/weight, density, icon_size). Use presets.list first if the user wants a complete restyle. For already-published pages, this saves an unpublished latest revision only. Do not call page.publish in

tools.name: page.meta.update
tools.description: Update internal page title, URL slug, and SEO title/description. For already-published pages, this saves an unpublished latest revision only. Do not call page.publish in the same assistant turn after this edit. Stop and tell the user the draft/preview was updated, then wait for a separate user messa

tools.name: page.preview
tools.description: Return the current preview URL for a page.

tools.name: page.publish
tools.description: Publish the latest page revision. Call this only when the user's latest message explicitly asks to publish, make the page live, or get a public share link. Do not call this immediately after page.create or page.create_from_brief just because the draft is publish-ready. For anonymous demo pages, incl

tools.name: page.unpublish
tools.description: Remove a published landing page from public availability while keeping its draft and revision history.

tools.name: block.update
tools.description: Patch one content block by stable blockId. Provide a props patch (merged into existing props) and/or a new type. Block types and props are validated against the strict per-type schema. For already-published pages, this saves an unpublished latest revision only. Do not call page.publish in the same a

tools.name: block.delete
tools.description: Delete one block by stable blockId. Use page.get first if unsure which id to remove. For already-published pages, this saves an unpublished latest revision only. Do not call page.publish in the same assistant turn after this edit. Stop and tell the user the draft/preview was updated, then wait for a

tools.name: block.reorder
tools.description: Move a block to a new zero-based index in the page main slot. For already-published pages, this saves an unpublished latest revision only. Do not call page.publish in the same assistant turn after this edit. Stop and tell the user the draft/preview was updated, then wait for a separate user message

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "block.add" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Append a new content block to a link-in-bio page. The block must match one of the 13 allowed types (link, social_row, whatsapp, phone_call, email_contact, image…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "page.publish" stacks urgency/override words (immediately, always). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: account.whoami, source.import, page.create, page.create_from_brief, page.claim, page.theme.update, page.publish, block.delete · Private data: source.import, page.create_from_brief · Public sink: page.create, page.create_from_brief, block.add, page.claim, page.header.update, page.theme.update, page.meta.update, page.publish, block.update, block.delete, block.reorder · Destructive: page.unpublish, block.delete.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: account.whoami, source.import, page.create, page.create_from_brief, page.claim, page.theme.update, page.publish, block.delete · Private data: source.import, page.create_from_brief · Public sink: page.create, page.create_from_brief, block.add, page.claim, page.header.update, page.theme.update, page.meta.update, page.publish, block.update, block.delete, block.reorder · Destructive: page.unpublish, block.delete

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
